subtitle: The benchmark for modern daiginjo, polished to 23%.
body:
Dassai 23 is the flagship of Asahi Shuzo in Yamaguchi, made with Yamada Nishiki rice polished down to 23%. The result is an intensely fragrant sake with notes of apple, banana, and lychee, yet a crisp, clean finish.

This sake revolutionised the industry by proving that high polishing could produce a consistent, fruitГўВҖВ‘forward style that appeals globally. It is best enjoyed slightly chilled.

tasting_notes: Crisp and dry with a subtle rice aroma. The flavour is clean, with hints of melon and a touch of almond. MediumГўВҖВ‘bodied with a sharp, refreshing finish. Excellent as a table sake.

subtitle: YamagataГўВҖВҷs legendary sake that redefined premium brewing.
body:
Juyondai, brewed by Takagi Shuzo, is one of the most soughtГўВҖВ‘after sake brands in Japan. Its rich, fruity profile and velvety texture come from the use of rare yeast strains and meticulous brewing techniques.

The signature label, "Juyondai" (ГҘВҚВҒГҘВӣВӣГӨВ»ВЈ), refers to the 14th generation of the Takagi family. This sake often features notes of melon, pear, and white flowers, with a long, elegant finish.

tasting_notes: A bold, savoury sake with aromas of roasted nuts, yoghurt, and a hint of mushroom. On the palate, itГўВҖВҷs fullГўВҖВ‘bodied with a sharp acidity that cuts through fatty fish. The finish is long and dry.

title_en: Kubota Manju ГўВҖВ” The Elegance of Niigata
subtitle: A flagship sake from the heart of Niigata, known for its clean, crisp profile.
body:
Kubota Manju is the pinnacle of Asahi ShuzoГўВҖВҷs brewing, representing the tanrei karakuchi style that Niigata is famous for. With a light, dry character and subtle fruit notes, it pairs perfectly with delicate seafood.

This sake is brewed using Gohyakumangoku rice, polished to 50%, and fermented at low temperatures to develop its refined aroma. The name "Manju" (ГЁВҗВ¬ГҘВҜВҝ) means "ten thousand longevities," symbolizing a wish for enduring happiness.

tasting_notes: Light and refreshing with subtle aromas of green apple and white flowers. The palate is smooth with a gentle sweetness balanced by a crisp, clean finish. A textbook Niigata ginjo.

subtitle: A labourГўВҖВ‘intensive method that yields complex, earthy flavours.
body:
Kimoto is the traditional yeastГўВҖВ‘starter method that predates modern additions of lactic acid. Brewers literally grind the rice mixture with poles to encourage wild lactic acid bacteria, a process that takes weeks but results in sake with deep umami and a robust character.

Today, only a handful of breweries still use the full kimoto method, valuing its ability to create distinctive, ageГўВҖВ‘worthy sake.

title_en: Sake and Food Pairing: A Guide
subtitle: How to match different styles of sake with dishes.
body:
Unlike wine, sakeГўВҖВҷs savoury umami and low acidity allow it to complement a wide range of cuisines. Light, fragrant daiginjo pairs well with delicate sashimi, while rich junmai stands up to grilled meats and cheese.

This guide explores the basic principles: match flavour intensity, consider regional pairings, and experiment with contrasting elements.

subtitle: The rustic, wild cousin of kimoto.
body:
Yamahai (ГҘВұВұГҘВ»Вғ) is a shortened form of yama-oroshi haishi, a method that skips the labourГўВҖВ‘intensive grinding of kimoto but still relies on natural lactic acid. It produces sake with pronounced acidity, gamey notes, and a fullГўВҖВ‘bodied texture.

Once seen as difficult to control, yamahai is now celebrated for the complexity it brings to junmai and ginjo styles.

title_en: Kokuryu Ishidaya ГўВҖВ” Mystery of Echizen
